description: You are an Air Quality Analyst investigating how weather drives Ozone formation. Using the built-in `airquality` dataset in RStudio, compare a Linear Regression model and a Generalized Additive Model (GAM) from the `mgcv` package, predicting `Ozone` from `Solar.R`, `Wind`, and `Temp`. Ensure the data is cleaned of missing values before modeling. Save your analysis code to `/home/ga/RProjects/ozone_analysis.R`. Export a comparison of the models' AIC values to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/model_comparison.csv` with columns `model` (containing 'lm' and 'gam') and `AIC`. Save a visualization of the GAM's partial effects to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/gam_smooths.png`. Finally, use the GAM to predict the Ozone level for a 'High Risk Day' (Solar.R=200, Wind=5, Temp=90) and save the numeric prediction to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/high_risk_prediction.txt`.
